title: St. George Forane Church Kallody, Wayanad
tagline: St. George Forane Church Kallody, Wayanad — spiritual town of Kerala

heroImage.alt: St. George Forane Church Kallody, Wayanad, Kerala

overview.short: St. George Forane Church Kallody, Wayanad is a much-loved spiritual town in Kerala, known for temples and pilgrimage.
overview.description: St. George Forane Church Kallody is situated in Mananthavady taluk of Wayanad district. It is one of the important churches and pilgrim centers in the region, in communion with the Syro-Malabar Catholic Church.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Kallody
topPlaces.description: Kallody is a village in the Edavaka Grama panchayath of the Wayanad district, Kerala, India. It forms part of the Mananthavady Taluk.

topPlaces.name: Tharuvana
topPlaces.description: Tharuvana is a village in Vellamunda Panchayath of Wayanad district, Kerala, India. The place is a junction connecting the Vythiri-Tharuvana road to State Highway 54 running between Kuttiady and Mananthavady.

topPlaces.name: Karingari
topPlaces.description: Karingari is a village near Tharuvana in Vellamunda panchayath, Mananthavady area, Wayanad, Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Mazhuvannur Maha Siva Kshethram
topPlaces.description: Mazhuvannur Maha Siva Kshethram is an ancient Hindu Temple at Tharuvana in Wayanad district of the Indian state of Kerala. The presiding deity is Lord Shiva; other deities are Arayil Bhagavathy, Shree Durga, Nandhi, Lord Ayyappa and Lord Ganapathi.

topPlaces.name: Mazhuvannur
topPlaces.description: Mazhuvannur (Literal meaning : The place where Axe has Fallen) is where many Hindus believe the axe of Shiva is believed to have fallen ) Mazhuvannur is a hilly village located 10 Kilometres from Mananthavady in Wayanad district, Kerala, India. The place is located near the small town of Tharuvana in Porunnannur Village.

topPlaces.name: Vimala Nagar, Wayanad
topPlaces.description: Vimala Nagar or Vimalanagar is a small village in the grama panchayat of Thavinjal, in Mananthavady taluk, Wayanad district, Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Thalappuzha, Wayanad
topPlaces.description: Thalappuzha, Thavinhal or Thavinjal is a village in Wayanad district in the state of Kerala, India. It is formally known as Aranguthum Chall, meaning the place for cultural programs.

topPlaces.name: Mananthavady
topPlaces.description: Mananthavady is a Municipality, Taluk and town in the Wayanad district of Kerala, India. The municipal boundaries are defined by Tirunelli Panchayat to the north, Kabini River to the east and south, and Thavinhal Panchayat to the west.

items.note: Kallody is a village in the Edavaka Grama panchayath of the Wayanad district, Kerala, India. It forms part of the Mananthavady Taluk.

Tharuvana is a village in Vellamunda Panchayath of Wayanad district, Kerala, India. The place is a junction connecting the Vythiri-Tharuvana road to State Highway 54 running between Kuttiady and Mananthavady.
Karingari is a village near Tharuvana in Vellamunda panchayath, Mananthavady area, Wayanad, Kerala, India.

itinerary.title: Day 2 — Mazhuvannur Maha Siva Kshethram & around

items.note: Mazhuvannur Maha Siva Kshethram is an ancient Hindu Temple at Tharuvana in Wayanad district of the Indian state of Kerala. The presiding deity is Lord Shiva; other deities are Arayil Bhagavathy, Shree Durga, Nandhi, Lord Ayyappa and Lord Ganapathi.

Mazhuvannur (Literal meaning : The place where Axe has Fallen) is where many Hindus believe the axe of Shiva is believed to have fallen ) Mazhuvannur is a hilly village located 10 Kilometres from Mananthavady in Wayanad district, Kerala, India. The place is located near the small town of Tharuvana in Porunnannur Village.
Vimala Nagar or Vimalanagar is a small village in the grama panchayat of Thavinjal, in Mananthavady taluk, Wayanad district, Kerala, India.
Thalappuzha, Thavinhal or Thavinjal is a village in Wayanad district in the state of Kerala, India. It is formally known as Aranguthum Chall, meaning the place for cultural programs.

Mananthavady is a Municipality, Taluk and town in the Wayanad district of Kerala, India. The municipal boundaries are defined by Tirunelli Panchayat to the north, Kabini River to the east and south, and Thavinhal Panchayat to the west.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit St. George Forane Church Kallody, Wayanad?
faq.a: The best time to visit St. George Forane Church Kallody, Wayanad is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach St. George Forane Church Kallody, Wayanad?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Calicut International Airport, Kozhikode (70 km) and the nearest railway station is Mysuru Junction (95 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for St. George Forane Church Kallody, Wayanad?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of St. George Forane Church Kallody, Wayanad at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in St. George Forane Church Kallody, Wayanad?
faq.a: Stays in St. George Forane Church Kallody, Wayanad start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in St. George Forane Church Kallody, Wayanad?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kallody, Tharuvana, Karingari, Mazhuvannur Maha Siva Kshethram, Mazhuvannur.
